howItWorks.intro: This crypto tax calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Capital Gain = Sale Price - Purchase Price
Short-term Tax = Gain × Ordinary Income Tax Rate
Long-term Tax = Gain × (0%, 15%, or 20% depending on bracket)
Net Profit = Capital Gain - Tax Owed
howItWorks.outro: Cryptocurrency markets are highly volatile. Past performance does not guarantee future results.

faq.question: How is cryptocurrency taxed in the US?
faq.answer: The IRS treats crypto as property. Selling, trading, or spending crypto triggers capital gains tax. Short-term gains (held < 1 year) are taxed as ordinary income. Long-term gains get preferential rates of 0%, 15%, or 20%.

faq.question: Do I owe tax on crypto I haven't sold?
faq.answer: No. Unrealized gains (crypto you still hold) are not taxable. Tax is only triggered when you sell, trade crypto-to-crypto, or spend crypto on goods/services.

faq.question: Can I offset crypto losses against gains?
faq.answer: Yes. Crypto losses can offset crypto gains dollar-for-dollar. Excess losses can offset up to $3,000 of ordinary income per year, with remaining losses carried forward.

faq.question: Is the Crypto Tax Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard cryptocurrency and blockchain formulas trusted by crypto investors and traders. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

sections.title: What Is the Crypto Tax Calculator?
sections.body: The Crypto Tax Calculator is a free online tool designed for crypto investors and traders who need quick, accurate calculations in the cryptocurrency and blockchain space. By entering your total purchase price, total sale price, holding period, you get instant results including capital gain, applicable tax rate, estimated tax. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting capital gain right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In cryptocurrency and blockchain, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by crypto investors and traders worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to evaluate your crypto portfolio with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your total purchase price and need to find the right capital gain. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Total Purchase Price ($)
howToSteps.description: Start by entering your total purchase price — this is the primary input for the calculation.

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: total sale price, holding period, income tax bracket. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your capital gain, applicable tax rate, estimated tax. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your total purchase price before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
